rationale: The confirmed US-Iran ceasefire announcement with Hormuz reopening and already-falling oil prices is the most relevant event for Portugal — lower energy costs benefit the Portuguese economy and cost of living.

top_events.title: US-Iran ceasefire announced; oil prices slide on Hormuz reopening
top_events.summary: Confirmed ceasefire and Strait of Hormuz reopening announcement is a qualitative step beyond prior expectations — oil prices already falling, which could ease Portugal's energy and transport costs. Full effect pending Friday signing.

top_events.title: UK and Japan agree £18bn investment deal
top_events.summary: A major UK-Japan investment deal has limited direct impact on Portugal but signals continued global trade activity relevant to EU economic context.

top_events.title: Tourist train overturns injuring 17 at Cártama tapas festival
top_events.summary: A tourist train accident in nearby Spain (Cártama, Málaga) is geographically close and relevant to Iberian tourism safety awareness, though limited policy impact.
